steps.title: Equate the exponents
steps.content:
Apply the power rule:
$$
p^{\frac{4}{5}}=p^{\frac{6(2n-1)}{5}}
$$
Since $p>1$, the exponents are equal:
$$
\frac{4}{5}=\frac{6(2n-1)}{5}
$$

steps.title: Solve for n
steps.content: Multiply by $5$ and solve: $$ 4=6(2n-1) $$ $$ 4=12n-6 $$ $$ 10=12n $$ The answer is 5/6.
